     Which of the following best describes the student’s calculation?

     A The calculation is completely correct.

     B The calculation is incorrect, and the ﬁrst error occurs on line (I).

     C The calculation is incorrect, and the ﬁrst error occurs on line (II).

     D The calculation is incorrect, and the ﬁrst error occurs on line (III).

     E The calculation is incorrect, and the ﬁrst error occurs on line (IV).

     F The calculation is incorrect, and the ﬁrst error occurs on line (V).

stem.rawText:
The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus (FTC) tells us that for any polynomial f :

                                         d
                                             CB x          D
                                        dx     0
                                                    f(t) dt = f(x)


                           d
                               CB 2x 2 D
     A student calculates dx     x
                                    t dt as follows:

                B 2x 2      B 2x 2      Bx 2
         (I)     x
                    t  dt =  0
                                t  dt −  0
                                           t dt


                         d
                               CB x 2 D
         (II)   By FTC, dx       0
                                   t dt = x2


                        d
                               CB 2x 2 D
         (III) By FTC, dx        0
                                    t dt = (2x)2 = 4x2

                        CB 2x 2 D
         (IV)       d
                So dx     x
                             t dt = 4x2 − x2


                        d
                            CB 2x 2 D
         (V)    giving dx     x
                                 t dt = 3x2


     Which of the following best describes the student’s calculation?

options.label: B
options.rawText: The calculation is incorrect, and the ﬁrst error occurs on line (I).

options.label: C
options.rawText: The calculation is incorrect, and the ﬁrst error occurs on line (II).

options.label: D
options.rawText: The calculation is incorrect, and the ﬁrst error occurs on line (III).

options.label: E
options.rawText: The calculation is incorrect, and the ﬁrst error occurs on line (IV).

options.label: F
options.rawText: The calculation is incorrect, and the ﬁrst error occurs on line (V).

solution.rawText:
We can check whether the calculation stands any chance of being correct by checking whether
line (V) is correct. We can work out the integral directly:
                                Z 2x      
                              d         2        d  1 3 2x 
                                       t dt =            t
                             dx    x            dx 3 x
                                                 d 1
                                                       (2x)3 − 13 x3
                                                                     
                                             =       3
                                                dx
                                                 d 7 3
                                             =         x
                                                dx 3
                                             = 7x2

So an error has certainly been introduced somewhere.
From this, it is clear that line (IV) is also incorrect.
                        Rb          Rc           Rc
Line (I) is correct, as a f(t) dt + b f(t) dt = a f(t) dt for all functions f(t) and real numbers a,
b and c.
Therefore the error must lie in line (II) or (III). We can work out the left hand sides of the
equations explicitly as we did for line (V):
                                      Z x     
                                   d         2       d  1 3 x 
                                           t dt =         t
                                  dx     0          dx 3 0
                                                     d 1 3
                                                 =       x )
                                                    dx 3
                                                 = x2

so line (II) is correct. However,
                                         Z 2x          
                                     d           2             d  1 3 2x 
                                                 t dt       =        t
                                    dx     0                  dx 3 0
                                                               d 8 3
                                                            =      x
                                                              dx 3
                                                            = 8x2

so line (III) is incorrect. Hence the correct option is D.
The reason for the error is that the fundamental theorem of calculus is stated for the case where
the upper limit is x; if the
                          R 2xupperlimit is 2x, then the integral grows twice as fast as x increases,
                      d
so we actually have dx 0 t dt = 2(2x) = 8x2 .
